title: Askellia pseudonaniformis
constituents: I don't have information about the active constituents of Askellia pseudonaniformis.
intro: Askellia pseudonaniformis, also known as Kandagattha, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, headache, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Askellia pseudonaniformis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Askellia pseudonaniformis is commonly used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and powders to treat various health conditions, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
